nativeRange.habitat: Tropical West and Central African freshwater rivers and streams, particularly in the lowland forest belt of Cameroon, where it grows rooted in fine substrate along clear flowing watercourses

decorPreferences: fine gravel or nutrient-rich substrate (avoid coarse or heavy gravel)
open background space tall enough for long arching leaves
leave at least the top half of the bulb exposed above the substrate

goodWith: plant-nibbling fish (tough leaves are generally ignored by herbivores)

summary: A striking West African bulb plant with very long, narrow, distinctly curly dark-green leaves; slow-growing background or centrepiece for medium-to-large planted tanks.
careNotes: Crinum calamistratum is a West African (notably Cameroon) freshwater bulb plant of the family Amaryllidaceae, instantly recognisable by its very long, narrow, deeply crinkled or curly dark-green leaves. Leaves are typically reported at 40 cm in juvenile plants and can reach 100–120 cm (around 1 m or more) in well-established specimens (Tropica, Aqua-Fish.net), so this is best placed at the back of medium-to-large tanks where its arching foliage has room to develop. PLANTING IS CRITICAL: the onion-shaped bulb must NEVER be fully buried — bury only the bottom half (roughly the lower 1/2 to 2/3) of the bulb and keep the upper portion exposed above the substrate; burying the whole bulb causes it to rot. Use a fine, nutrient-rich gravel substrate (heavy or coarse gravel and very deep sand are discouraged) and supplement with root tabs, as it is a heavy root feeder. Light demand is given as low by Tropica but most aquarium retailers and care guides (Aquarium Co-Op, Aqua-Fish.net, Steenfott Aquatics) recommend medium-to-high lighting for healthy growth and possible flowering; the average sits around medium, and the plant tolerates a wide range. CO2 is not strictly required (Tropica rates demand low) but supplementation is generally reported to improve leaf density and growth rate. Temperature reports cluster around 22–28 °C (averaged here at 22–27 °C); pH 6.0–7.8 and a broad hardness range (0–18 dGH) are well tolerated. It is slow to establish and reacts poorly to transplantation and sudden water-chemistry shifts — once settled it should be left undisturbed. The leaves are tough and reportedly resistant to plant-eating fish (goldfish, large cichlids), making it one of the few options for tanks with plant-nibblers. Tropica also notes tolerance of low brackish salt concentrations, though it is primarily a freshwater species.
breedingNotes: Propagates vegetatively by small daughter bulbs ('bulb shoots') that develop from the base of an established mother bulb; these can be separated and replanted once they have their own roots and leaves. Mature plants under strong lighting may produce showy white flowers on tall emergent stalks, though this is uncommon in typical aquarium setups.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Tropica species page for Crinum calamistratum. Origin West Africa, height 40–120 cm, light demand low, CO2 demand low, growth rate slow, dark-green narrow curly leaves, forms small bulbs, tolerates low brackish salinity, resistant to herbivorous fish. Manifest-supplied search URL (https://tropica.com/en/search?q=Crinum+calamistratum) returned only navigation/footer; resolved to the canonical plantdetails page via site search.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Secondary source. Confirmed bulb plant with very long, narrow, dark-green crinkly leaves; extremely slow growing; background or centerpiece placement; at least medium lighting; easy/beginner-friendly care designation.

additional.name: Aqua-Fish.net
additional.notes: Used for water parameters where Tropica is silent: temperature 20–27 °C, pH 6.1–7.8, hardness 0–18 dGH. Confirmed height 40–120 cm, slow growth, family Amaryllidaceae, origin Cameroon, propagation by basal bulb shoots, leave part of bulb exposed to prevent rot, medium difficulty.

additional.name: Aqualand Pets Plus — Crinum Calamistratum
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature 20–28 °C (68–82 °F), pH 6.1–7.9, explicit half-bury rule (only ~2/3 of bulb in substrate, ~1/3 exposed; fully buried = rot), fine gravel substrate, heavy root feeder, medium-to-high lighting, poor transplantation tolerance.

additional.name: Steenfott Aquatics — Crinum Calamistratum
additional.notes: Used for temperature 23–26 °C, lighting 8–10 hours per day moderate-to-high, slow growth, leaves up to ~4 ft / ~120 cm, native to west/central Africa especially Cameroon, generally ignored by plant-eating fish, can flower under bright light in soft slightly acidic water.
